title: Union Find (Disjoint Set)
description: Master union-find data structure for connectivity problems

questions.question: What are the two main operations in Union Find?
questions.explanation: Union Find supports Find (find set representative) and Union (merge two sets) operations.

questions.question: What is path compression in Union Find?
options: Making nodes point directly to root during Find
questions.explanation: Path compression flattens the tree by making every node on the path point directly to the root during Find.

questions.question: What is the amortized time complexity with both optimizations?
questions.explanation: With path compression and union by rank, operations take O(α(n)) time, where α is inverse Ackermann (effectively constant).

questions.question: What does union by rank do?
options: Attaches smaller tree under larger tree
questions.explanation: Union by rank attaches the tree with smaller rank under the tree with larger rank, keeping trees shallow.

problems.title: Number of Provinces
problems.description:
There are n cities. Some of them are connected, while some are not. If city a is connected directly with city b, and city b is connected directly with city c, then city a is connected indirectly with city c.

A province is a group of directly or indirectly connected cities and no other cities outside of the group.

You are given an n x n matrix isConnected where isConnected[i][j] = 1 if the ith city and the jth city are directly connected, and isConnected[i][j] = 0 otherwise.

Return the total number of provinces.

problems.title: Redundant Connection
problems.description:
In this problem, a tree is an undirected graph that is connected and has no cycles.

You are given a graph that started as a tree with n nodes labeled from 1 to n, with one additional edge added. The added edge has two different vertices chosen from 1 to n, and was not an edge that already existed.

Return an edge that can be removed so that the resulting graph is a tree of n nodes.

hiddenTestCases.description: No redundant edge (shouldn't happen per constraints)

commonMistakes: Not using path compression - makes Find operations slow
Not using union by rank/size - creates tall trees
Forgetting to find roots before comparing - must call find() on both elements
Modifying parent array directly - always use union() to merge sets
Using Union Find for directed graphs - it's designed for undirected connectivity
patternDetection: Problem asks about connectivity or grouping
